insight: Endotherm Kit is most effective in high-economy sets where there are large absorbers like Centurion that can be exploited for absorb-denial freezes; popping frostbites to kill Rhinos is inefficient, and holding 1-charge Rhinos for defense is valuable.
author: mtanzer
context: Discussion of advanced unit strategies and set-dependent decision-making.

insight: In base+Antima Comet sets, the optimal opening depends heavily on soak availability; with good soak (Infusion Grid, Plexo, etc.), slower Antima is better, and players should not fear waiting to buy Engineers over multiple turns rather than rushing the comet.
author: mtanzer
context: Analysis of Antima Comet timing decisions based on set composition.

insight: Centrifuge (post-patch) does not generate enough gold to justify T2 purchase for expensive red/blue units like Vai Mauronax, Odin, or Lucent Hellion; slower Centrifuge into tech-heavy plays avoids floating resources.
author: mtanzer
context: Discussion of when to buy Centrifuge based on available red/blue sinks.

insight: Savior is viable in P1 if there is careful early defense (e.g., via Tarsiers vs Walls), but P2 T3 Savior is especially vulnerable to early rushes and requires either very comfortable defense soak or good absorbers to justify the purchase.
author: mtanzer
context: Analysis of when Savior can be rushed and defended against.

insight: Choosing the number of Thorium Dynamos is similar to choosing drone count—aim for the right economy size; in sets where green is weak, Dynamo is still viable for gold generation via its click ability and costs less energy than Drones.
author: mtanzer
context: Discussion of Dynamo economy in various set compositions.

insight: Evil Animus (Animus into Vortex/Botch) is stronger when there are additional synergies like Phage or supporting units, and weak if rushed too fast without red sinks in sets with only Vortex and Botch.
author: mtanzer
context: Analysis of Evil Animus in different set contexts.

insight: Tia Threnody early play depends heavily on set composition; if Tia is the only green sink and better soak/absorb exists, letting P2 set up for Tia is acceptable. Threatening Tia with an early Blastforge can deter naked Tia while maintaining your own Tia option.
author: mtanzer
context: Analysis of Tia Threnody early-game decision trees.

insight: The DD/CC/DD/Fabrikator opening is viable only in specific conditions: set must clearly be Fabricator-friendly, the line cannot be punished, and the set must not be high-econ; this line is vulnerable to fast attack units like Tesla.
author: mtanzer
context: Discussion of Fabricator opening viability conditions.

insight: Colossus justifies a third Engineer in base+Colossus because Colossus easily beats Tarsiers; however, if there are good counters like absorb denial or chill, third Engineer becomes questionable.
author: mtanzer
context: Analysis of when Colossus justifies increased tech spending.

insight: Cryo Ray often justifies multiple Conduits because it gains extra value from granularity freezes and freezing absorbers; when both players build Cryo Rays, try to buy more Engineers and maintain backup absorbers to avoid exploitation.
author: mtanzer
context: Analysis of Cryo Ray value and response strategies.

insight: In Galvani+Antima sets, one cannot blindly rush Galvani+Antima every game by committing heavily to Engineers and Galvanis; the correct approach depends on set composition and whether you can sink the tech afterward to 'drone up' if opponent clears Galvanis.
author: mqp
context: Discussion of Galvani+Antima strategy and when to commit to the rushing plan.

insight: In Chrono+Galvani+Antima sets, Chrono is the only strong reason to play 1. 11EE (1 Engineer + 1 Galvani) on turn 1; without Chrono, a normal 1. DD opening is superior.
author: mrguy888
context: Analysis of turn-1 acceleration in Chrono+Galvani+Antima.

insight: In Chrono+Galvani+Antima, P2's fastest Antima rush (DDC → DDC2 → Antima) is strong because P2 gets ~13 Engineers and crushes P1's economy; P2 should fire the Antima to stop P1 from building any offense, then ride the economic advantage.
author: mqp
context: Deep analysis of P2 Antima rush in Chrono+Galvani+Antima.

insight: Wincer rush is much harder to execute quickly than Hannibull rush; Hannibull can come online T2-T3 with Galvani support, while Wincer requires 13+ tech worth of setup, giving P1 more time to defend or build threatening units.
author: extratricky
context: Debate on Chrono+Galvani+Hannibull+Wincer as an autowin combo.

insight: Hannibull in sets with Chrono+Galvani+Wincer can function like a defensive wall-breaker and econ accelerator (similar to Thunderhead value), not just as a pure attacker; this dual role makes it harder to counter than raw attack.
author: leary93
context: Discussion of Hannibull's versatility in mixed attack/defense roles.

insight: Deadeye Operative can contest Wincer rush by coming out earlier; the P1 line DD DD DB1 2B allows Deadeyes to snipe drones while P2 commits to Chrono+Wincer, resulting in a close game if P1 plays sharply.
author: leary93
context: Analysis of P1 anti-Wincer lines using Deadeye.

insight: Mirroring Hannibull in Chrono+Galvani+Hannibull sets often results in one player winning decisively because one player ends up frontlining 2 attack from the other; attack-based counters (Pixie, Immolite) are more reliable than pure soak.
author: extratricky
context: Analysis of Hannibull mirror matchups and alternative counter strategies.

insight: Wincer does not have to fire immediately when it arrives; if the opponent has no permanent attack, Wincer can pass and build economy, forcing the opponent to either build attack (losing drone production) or allow the Wincer to eventually attack.
author: extratricky
context: Discussion of Wincer timing and decision-making.

insight: Against Wincer, you can sometimes gambit (intentionally allow an attack) if you believe the opponent cannot afford a Wincer shot that immediately wins the game; this forces them to delay the shot or lose the unit.
author: wilson0862
context: Discussion of advanced Wincer counterplay using gambit tactics.

insight: Mahar Rectifier is a large absorber; if planning to use Mahar as primary absorb, the opening should be DD DDE to establish economy before committing to Mahar, not rushing Mahar early.
author: mrguy888
context: Analysis of Mahar Rectifier in a specific +5 set.
